name: Early Recognition Hunger
description: Early experiences taught the person that being seen and being valuable are tightly connected.
life_effect: Future choices may repeatedly seek stages where worth can be proven.

name: Failure Memory Lock
description: A past failure or near-failure has become a reference point for future risk.
life_effect: The person may overpay for certainty because uncertainty feels historically dangerous.

name: Stable Role Trap
description: A stable role has become both a shelter and a cage.
life_effect: The person may need a staged exit because the old role still protects real needs.

name: Relationship Mirror History
description: Relationships have repeatedly reflected a pattern the person struggles to see alone.
life_effect: External observation becomes a key route out of private self-simulation.

name: Unlived Self Accumulation
description: An unlived self has accumulated enough pressure to start shaping present decisions.
life_effect: If not given a small channel, this pressure may eventually seek a large rupture.
